author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
e mana! tumi vā bhulecha kise
tomāre dekhiyā, śamana⋅kiṅkara,
hāte tāli diyā hāse

rātri-dine kata, asata pacālo,
‘śrī⋅hari’ kahite nāro
emana durlabha, janama pāiye,
ki sukhe e kṣepa hāro

dhana⋅jane jata, āpanā balicha,
ke tora jāibe sāthe
gāyera gumāne, pichu nā gaṇili,
ṭhekāli śamana⋅hāte

dekhiye śuniye, bujhite nārili,
asāre jānili sāra
āpanāra māthā, āpani bhāṅgili,
bala nā e doṣa kā’ra

ekhana takhana, kakhana ki jāni,
hāsite khelite paḍi
e sukha smaribe, galāya jakhana,
caḍibe cāmera daḍi

vadana bhariyā, ‘hari hari’ bala,
śamana taribe sukhe
kahe premānanda, ‘hari’ nā bhajili,
kāli cuṇa tora mukhe
en_translation:
O my mind! What have you forgotten? Seeing your condition, the servants of the God of Death clap their hands and laugh.

Day and night you waste your time in useless talk and can never utter "Śrī Hari." Having obtained such a rare human birth, for what supposed pleasure do you lose this opportunity?

Of all the wealth and people you consider your own, who among them will go with you in the end? In the pride of your body, you did not consider the end, and now you have fallen into the hands of death.

Even after seeing and hearing the truth, you could not understand it; you considered the temporary to be essential. You have brought this ruin upon yourself—tell me, whose fault is it?

Who knows when, whether now or then, you will suddenly fall while laughing and playing? You will remember your current pleasures when the rope of the God of Death tightens around your neck.

Chant "Hari! Hari!" with a full heart and you will happily cross over the kingdom of death. Premānanda says: "If you do not worship Lord Hari, there will be nothing but disgrace upon your face."
